info.name: IdenVault API Collection
info.description:
Layer 2 — Backend API Testing (20 core cases)
Layer 5 — Security Testing, Newman subset (5 cases)
Supplementary — Templates and Admin (5 cases)

Total: 30 requests
Student: Pharthiban A/L Kumarhesan (1211112294)

PRE-RUN CHECKLIST (every single run):
1. Backend running on port 3001
2. Run generate-test-sig.js FRESH — paste issuer_wallet and issuer_sig into environment
3. Re-export Postman environment JSON to postman/IdenVault_Dev_Environment.json
4. Run Newman immediately — do not wait or call /api/auth/nonce again between steps 2 and 4

NOTE on ref_ids:
CRED-01 and CRED-04 now generate timestamp-based ref_ids via pre-request scripts.
This prevents duplicate key constraint violations across multiple Newman runs.

NOTE on expired_token (QR-03, SEC-06):
After the Newman run completes:
1. Run QR-01 manually in Postman GUI to get a token
2. Wait 30+ seconds
3. Paste the token into the expired_token environment variable
4. Run QR-03 and SEC-06 manually and screenshot the 401 TOKEN_EXPIRED responses

item.name: Authentication
item.description:
4 cases: API-AUTH-01 to API-AUTH-04
Execution order: 02 → 04 → 03 → 01 (nonce collision avoidance)
IMPORTANT: Run generate-test-sig.js before EVERY Newman run.
The nonce is consumed by AUTH-03 and must be freshly generated each time.

request.description:
TC: API-AUTH-02
Input: No wallet query param
Expected: 400 — { error: 'wallet required' }
Does NOT affect the issuer_wallet nonce in server memory.

TC: API-AUTH-04
Input: Valid wallet + deliberately zeroed/invalid signature
Expected: 401 — signature verification failed
NOTE: A failed verify does NOT consume the stored nonce — AUTH-03 can still use it.

TC: API-AUTH-03
Input: issuer_wallet + issuer_sig (pre-set by generate-test-sig.js)
Expected: 200 — { authenticated: true, wallet, role }
NOTE: Consumes the nonce. Must re-run generate-test-sig.js before each Newman run.

exec: pm.test('API-AUTH-01: Nonce is a non-empty string', function() {

request.description:
TC: API-AUTH-01
Input: Valid Ethereum wallet address as query param
Expected: 200 — { nonce: string }
NOTE: Runs LAST in this folder after AUTH-03 has consumed the signing nonce.

item.name: Credentials
item.description:
12 cases: API-CRED-01 to API-CRED-12
Execution order: 01 → 02 → 03 → 04 → 05 → 06 → 07 → 08 → 09 → 10 → 12 → 11

FIXES IN THIS VERSION:
- CRED-01: pre-request script generates timestamp-based {{cred_ref_id}} each run (no more duplicate key)
- CRED-04: pre-request script generates timestamp-based batch ref_ids each run
- CRED-07/08: removed fragile 'contains CRED-01' assertion — just checks status + array shape

item.name: API-CRED-01 — POST issue credential (issuer wallet, valid → 201)

exec: // Generate a unique ref_id each run to avoid duplicate key constraint violations

request.description:
TC: API-CRED-01
Header: x-wallet-address = issuer_wallet (trusted issuer)
Input: Valid credential payload — ref_id is timestamp-generated to avoid duplicate key errors
Expected: 201 — credential object returned
Sets: {{test_ref_id}} from body.ref_id (used by CRED-09, 10, 11, 12, QR-01, SEC-04)

exec: pm.test('API-CRED-02: Error mentions trusted issuer', function() {

request.description:
TC: API-CRED-02
Header: x-wallet-address = student_wallet (NOT a trusted issuer)
Expected: 403 — 'Not a trusted issuer'
NOTE: This request is rejected before the credential is saved, so no unique ref_id needed.

item.name: API-CRED-03 — POST issue credential (no auth header → 401)

request.description:
TC: API-CRED-03
Header: No x-wallet-address header
Expected: 401 — 'Valid wallet address required'
NOTE: Rejected by middleware before DB write — no unique ref_id needed.

exec: // Generate unique batch ref_ids each run to avoid duplicate key constraint violations
console.log('Generated batch ref_ids with ts:', ts);
pm.test('API-CRED-04: credentials is an array', function() {

request.description:
TC: API-CRED-04
Header: x-wallet-address = issuer_wallet
Input: 3 valid credential objects — ref_ids are timestamp-generated to avoid duplicate key errors
Expected: 201 — { credentials: [...], count: 3 }

item.name: API-CRED-05 — POST batch (51 entries → 400 max limit)

request.description:
TC: API-CRED-05
Header: x-wallet-address = issuer_wallet
Input: 51 credential objects (generated by pre-request script)
Expected: 400 — 'Maximum 50 credentials per batch'
NOTE: Rejected before DB write so unique ref_ids aren't strictly needed,
but the pre-request script uses timestamps anyway for consistency.

exec: pm.test('API-CRED-06: Error mentions required fields', function() {

request.description:
TC: API-CRED-06
Header: x-wallet-address = issuer_wallet
Input: Batch entry missing ref_id
Expected: 400 — 'Each credential must have ref_id, title, and holder_wallet'

exec: pm.test('API-CRED-07: credentials is an array', function() {
pm.test('API-CRED-07: Returns at least one credential', function() {

request.description:
TC: API-CRED-07
Header: x-wallet-address = issuer_wallet
Query: role=issuer
Expected: 200 — { credentials: [...] } — credentials issued by this wallet
FIX: Removed fragile 'contains CRED-01 credential' assertion.
Now just verifies the endpoint returns 200 with a non-empty credentials array.

exec: pm.test('API-CRED-08: credentials is an array', function() {
pm.test('API-CRED-08: Returns at least one credential', function() {

request.description:
TC: API-CRED-08
Header: x-wallet-address = student_wallet
Expected: 200 — { credentials: [...] } — credentials held by this wallet
FIX: Removed fragile 'contains CRED-01 credential' assertion.
Now just verifies the endpoint returns 200 with a non-empty credentials array.

item.name: API-CRED-09 — GET credential by refId (public, valid → 200 + blockchain)

exec: pm.test('API-CRED-09: blockchain has valid, revoked, issuer', function() {

request.description:
TC: API-CRED-09
No auth header — public endpoint
Input: Valid ref_id set by CRED-01 ({{test_ref_id}})
Expected: 200 — credential + blockchain: { valid, revoked, issuer } from live Infura/Sepolia call

TC: API-CRED-10
No auth header — public endpoint
Input: A ref_id that does not exist in Supabase
Expected: 404 — 'Credential not found'

item.name: API-CRED-12 — DELETE credential (student wallet → 403 Not the issuer)

exec: pm.test('API-CRED-12: Error mentions issuer ownership', function() {

request.description:
TC: API-CRED-12
Header: x-wallet-address = student_wallet (NOT the issuer)
Expected: 403 — 'Not the issuer'
NOTE: Runs before CRED-11 intentionally.

TC: API-CRED-11
Header: x-wallet-address = issuer_wallet
Expected: 200 — { revoked: true, refId }
NOTE: Supabase-side revocation only. On-chain revokeCredential() is done via MetaMask from the frontend.

item.name: Presentation and QR
item.description:
4 cases: API-QR-01 to API-QR-04
QR-01 generates a 30-second token using {{test_ref_id}} set by CRED-01.
QR-02 uses it immediately (must run within 30s of QR-01).
QR-03 requires a manually set {{expired_token}} — see request description.

exec: console.log('Set test_token. Valid for ~30s — QR-02 runs next.');

request.description:
TC: API-QR-01
Header: x-wallet-address = student_wallet (the credential holder)
Expected: 200 — { token: JWT, expiresAt }
Sets: {{test_token}} for QR-02
For {{expired_token}}: run this manually in Postman GUI after the Newman run, wait 30s+, paste into expired_token env var.

TC: API-QR-02
No auth — public endpoint
Input: { token } — valid JWT set by QR-01
Expected: 200 — full credential data + presentedBy

item.name: API-QR-03 — POST verify/token (expired token → 401 TOKEN_EXPIRED) [MANUAL SETUP]

exec: pm.test('API-QR-03: Error code is TOKEN_EXPIRED', function() {

request.description:
TC: API-QR-03
⚠️ MANUAL SETUP REQUIRED:
1. Run API-QR-01 in Postman GUI to get a fresh token
2. Copy the token value
3. Wait 30+ seconds for it to expire
4. Paste into {{expired_token}} environment variable
5. Run this request and screenshot the 401 TOKEN_EXPIRED response
Expected: 401 — { code: 'TOKEN_EXPIRED' }

TC: API-QR-04
No auth — public endpoint
Input: Empty body (no token field)
Expected: 400 — 'token is required'

item.name: Security — Newman
item.description:
5 Newman-automatable cases from Layer 5: SEC-01 to SEC-04, SEC-06
SEC-05 is covered by SC-CA-09 (Hardhat Layer 1).
SEC-07 (nonce replay) and SEC-08 (Supabase RLS) are manual tests.

item.name: SEC-01 — POST credentials (student wallet → 403 Not trusted issuer)

exec: pm.test('SEC-01: Error is Not a trusted issuer', function() {

request.description:
TC: SEC-01
Security boundary: student wallet cannot issue credentials
Expected: 403 — 'Not a trusted issuer'
NOTE: Rejected before DB write — no unique ref_id needed.

exec: pm.test('SEC-02: Error mentions wallet address', function() {

request.description:
TC: SEC-02
Security boundary: missing wallet header rejected by requireWallet middleware
Expected: 401 — 'Valid wallet address required'

exec: pm.test('SEC-03: Error mentions admin access', function() {

request.description:
TC: SEC-03
Security boundary: admin route rejects non-admin wallets
Expected: 403 — 'Forbidden: admin access required'

item.name: SEC-04 — DELETE credential (wrong wallet → 403 Not the issuer)

exec: pm.test('SEC-04: Error is Not the issuer', function() {

request.description:
TC: SEC-04
Security boundary: only the original issuer can revoke their credential
Expected: 403 — 'Not the issuer'
NOTE: Runs after CRED-11 has already revoked the credential in Supabase.
The record still exists so the ownership check still fires correctly.

item.name: SEC-06 — POST verify/token (expired token → 401 TOKEN_EXPIRED) [MANUAL SETUP]

exec: pm.test('SEC-06: Error code is TOKEN_EXPIRED', function() {

request.description:
TC: SEC-06
Security boundary: QR presentation tokens expire after 30 seconds
⚠️ Same MANUAL SETUP as QR-03: set {{expired_token}} before running.
Expected: 401 — { code: 'TOKEN_EXPIRED' }

item.name: Supplementary — Templates and Admin
item.description:
5 supplementary cases: API-TPL-01 to 03, API-ADM-01 to 02
NOT counted in the core 20 Layer 2 test cases.
All 5 passing as of v3.

exec: pm.test('API-TPL-01: Response has templates array', function() {
pm.test('API-TPL-01: Contains at least one template', function() {

request.description:
TC: API-TPL-01
Header: x-wallet-address = issuer_wallet
Expected: 200 — { templates: [...] } (system defaults + issuer custom templates)

TC: API-TPL-02
Header: x-wallet-address = issuer_wallet
Expected: 201 — { template: { id, title, ... } }
Sets: {{created_template_id}} for API-TPL-03

TC: API-TPL-03
Header: x-wallet-address = issuer_wallet
URL: Uses {{created_template_id}} set by API-TPL-02
Expected: 200 — CSV file with column headers matching template fields

TC: API-ADM-02
Header: x-wallet-address = student_wallet (not admin)
Expected: 403 — 'Forbidden: admin access required'
